title: pilosella rothiana subsp. carnuntii
intro: Pilosella rothiana subsp. carnuntii, also known as a subspecies of the common creaking-rush or creeping saxifrage, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the sunflower family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Pilosella rothiana subsp. carnuntii include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe skin irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves or roots in hot water to create teas, infusions, or decoctions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
